summary: Frontend smoke + targeted regression of 3 requested changes (product images, chat rename, checkout PayPal/card UI). All 15 targeted assertions PASS on live preview URL.

product_images.mcafee_premium_individual: PASS - all images from gosoftwarebuy.com/objects/uploads/, no unsplash, no broken images
product_images.bitdefender_premium_vpn: PASS - all images from gosoftwarebuy.com, real box-shot renders correctly (verified visually), no broken images

checkout_payment.card_inputs_present: PASS - card-number-input, card-exp-input, card-cvv-input all rendered
checkout_payment.paypal_selectable: PASS - clicking flips radio, redirect info text shown
checkout_payment.paypal_button_yellow_style: PASS - bg-[#FFC439] class applied; label shows 'PayPal · Continue $X'

test_results.console_errors: Only 10 x 401 from /api/auth/me as guest user (expected behavior, pre-existing, unrelated to changes)

critical_code_review_comments: None blocking. Minor: card fields in Checkout.jsx are decorative-only (real entry on Stripe hosted page). Consider adding a small helper note like 'These are placeholders — you will enter your card on the secure Stripe page' to reduce user confusion, since users may enter card data here expecting it to be used. Currently the small line '...tokenized securely via Stripe. We never store card details.' partially addresses this but does not say the local fields aren't submitted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 verified the 3 requested changes (real product images via gosoftwarebuy.com, chat renamed to 'Chat' everywhere, selectable PayPal + decorative card fields). Stripe redirect works for card method. PayPal selection switches button to yellow PayPal style. 401s on /api/auth/me are expected guest behavior.
